purpose.summary: The COBOL program PAUDBLOD is designed to insert child segments into an IMS database. It retrieves a root segment and then inserts a child segment related to that root, handling potential errors during the IMS calls.

inputs.name: INFILE1
inputs.description: First input file, purpose unknown from this code snippet.

inputs.name: INFILE2
inputs.description: Second input file, purpose unknown from this code snippet.

inputs.name: PAUTBPCB
inputs.description: IMS PCB (Program Communication Block) used for IMS database interaction.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY
inputs.description: Root segment to be retrieved from the IMS database.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
inputs.description: Child segment to be inserted into the IMS database.

inputs.name: ROOT-QUAL-SSA
inputs.description: SSA (Segment Search Argument) for qualifying the root segment retrieval.

inputs.name: CHILD-UNQUAL-SSA
inputs.description: SSA (Segment Search Argument) for the child segment insertion.

called_programs.purpose: Interface with IMS DL/I to perform database operations.
Interface with IMS DL/I to perform database operations.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph attempts to retrieve a root segment from the IMS database and, upon successful retrieval, proceeds to insert a child segment. It initializes PAUT-PCB-STATUS, then calls CBLTDLI with FUNC-GU to retrieve the root segment PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY using the ROOT-QUAL-SSA. After the call, it checks the PAUT-PCB-STATUS. If the status is spaces, indicating success, it calls 3200-INSERT-IMS-CALL to insert the child segment. If the status is not spaces or 'II', it displays an error message including the PAUT-PCB-STATUS and PAUT-KEYFB, and then performs 9999-ABEND to terminate the program. The paragraph uses DISPLAY statements for debugging and monitoring the IMS calls.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 3100-INSERT-CHILD-SEG paragraph. It contains a simple EXIT statement to allow control to return to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph inserts a child segment into the IMS database. It calls CBLTDLI with FUNC-ISRT to insert the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment using the CHILD-UNQUAL-SSA. After the call, it checks the PAUT-PCB-STATUS. If the status is spaces, it displays a success message. If the status is 'II', it displays a message indicating the segment is already in the database. If the status is neither spaces nor 'II', it displays an error message including the PAUT-PCB-STATUS and PAUT-KEYFB, and then performs 9999-ABEND to terminate the program. The paragraph uses DISPLAY statements for debugging and monitoring the IMS calls.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 3200-INSERT-IMS-CALL paragraph. It contains a simple EXIT statement to allow control to return to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph closes the input files INFILE1 and INFILE2. It displays a message indicating that the files are being closed. It then closes INFILE1 and checks the WS-INFIL1-STATUS. If the status is spaces or '00', it continues. Otherwise, it displays an error message including the WS-INFIL1-STATUS. It repeats this process for INFILE2 and WS-INFIL2-STATUS. This paragraph handles the closing of input files and checks for errors during the close operation.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 4000-FILE-CLOSE paragraph. It contains a simple EXIT statement to allow control to return to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph handles abnormal termination of the program. It displays a message indicating that the IMS load is abending. It then moves 16 to the RETURN-CODE and performs a GOBACK to terminate the program. This paragraph is called when an unrecoverable error occurs during the IMS processing.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 9999-ABEND paragraph. It contains a simple EXIT statement to allow control to return to the calling paragraph.

error_handling.condition: PAUT-PCB-STATUS NOT EQUAL TO SPACES AND 'II' (in 3100-INSERT-CHILD-SEG)
PAUT-PCB-STATUS NOT EQUAL TO SPACES AND 'II' (in 3200-INSERT-IMS-CALL)
WS-INFIL1-STATUS not equal to SPACES or '00'
WS-INFIL2-STATUS not equal to SPACES or '00'
